Scroll

Most of us carry more thoughts

than we can hold.

 

untangle gives you a place to speak them,

the wisdom to understand them,

and the clarity to live beyond them.

Who untangle is for

If any of these feel familiar,
you're in the right place.

01Stuck

Going in circles without knowing why

02Lost

Unsure who you are or where you're heading

03Overwhelmed

Too much in your head to think straight

04Unaware

Patterns you can't see from the inside

05Curious

Ready to understand yourself more deeply

Voice journaling

Think out loud.
Stop forcing words.

Tap once. Start talking. untangle listens, transcribes, and understands — automatically. No blank page. No typing. Just your voice, and everything it carries. Voice Activity Detection means you don't even need to press stop.

Start journaling free →
Daily summaries

Every day,
made sense of.

Five minutes after your last note, untangle distils your day into a structured summary — your thoughts, moods, ideas, decisions, even your meals. A unique piece of AI-generated artwork is created for every single day. Your journal becomes a living portrait.

See an example →

— Marcus Aurelius —

"What disturbs men's minds is not events but their judgments on events. Tell me — what judgment are you holding right now?"

I keep feeling like I'm failing, even when things go well...

"Then the failure is not in the events. It is in the story you have chosen to tell about them."

AI conversations

Choose your
guide.

Talk with Krishna, Buddha, Rumi, Carl Jung, Jiddu Krishnamurti, Ramana Maharishi, Adi Shankaracharya, Charlie Munger, Marcus Aurelius, and more. Voice or text. Each mind has a distinct philosophy and draws on your journal — so the conversation goes deeper than you expect.

Krishna Buddha Rumi Carl Jung Jiddu Krishnamurti Charlie Munger Ramana Maharishi Adi Shankaracharya Marcus Aurelius Socrates Audre Lorde

Your journal knows you
better than you think.

Deep insights

A portrait of who you are,
built from your own words.

Four insight layers that deepen over time — your personality, values, thinking patterns, and life story.

Big Five personality

Your dimensions

Derived from the language patterns in your journal — not a quiz.

Enneagram + character portrait

Type 4 — The Individualist

"You move through the world with a deep hunger for meaning — drawn to what is authentic, repelled by what feels hollow. You feel things fully, which is both your gift and your weight."

"I tend to feel things deeply""I am always searching"
Values constellation

What you return to

Scored by frequency and emotional intensity across your entries.

Authenticity Depth of connection Creative expression Independence Meaning over comfort Intellectual curiosity
Stated vs. actual divergence

Where attention actually goes

The most actionable insight — where what you say diverges from where you live.

You say you value

Rest and recovery

But your attention goes to

Output and achievement — 4.2× more often

Decision style

Intuition-first deliberator

You form strong initial impressions and build cases around them. Your best decisions happen when you honour the initial signal rather than override it with analysis.

Systems thinking

72

Blind spots

Patterns in your reasoning

Cognitive patterns detected across your writing over time.

Detected

Confirmation bias in planning stages

Detected

All-or-nothing framing under pressure

Detected

Future-self distancing when making commitments

Life arc

The Seeker

"Your arc is defined by movement — toward understanding, toward meaning, toward a version of yourself that feels fully inhabited. Each chapter ends not with resolution but with a deeper question."

Current chapter · since March 2025

"The Quiet Reformation"

Recurring cast

Who appears in your story

Unnamed archetypes that recur across your writing.

Archetype

The Challenger — appears in high-stakes decisions

Archetype

The Witness — the part of you that watches without judgment

Archetype

The Supporter — shows up when you're about to give up

Wisdom shorts

3,000+ ideas from philosophy, psychology,
neuroscience, and beyond.

Curated daily. Ranked to where you actually are right now.

Stoicism · Marcus Aurelius

"You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realise this, and you will find strength."

Overwhelmed
Neuroscience

Social rejection activates the same neural pathways as physical pain. The hurt is real — not just in your head.

Disconnected
Sufi mysticism · Rumi

The wound is the place where the light enters you.

Lost
Buddhism · Pema Chödrön

The most fundamental aggression to ourselves is to remain ignorant by not having the courage to look at ourselves honestly.

Unaware
Psychology · Carl Jung

Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.

Stuck
Philosophy · Aristotle

Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.

Nutrition science

Your gut produces 95% of your body's serotonin. What you eat shapes not just your body, but the chemistry of your mood.

Burned out
Feminist theory · Audre Lorde

Caring for myself is not self-indulgence. It is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare.

Self-critical
Christian mysticism · Simone Weil

Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.

Purposeless
Psychology · Viktor Frankl

When we are no longer able to change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves.

Stuck
Tibetan Buddhism

The present moment always will have been. Whatever goodness exists now is permanent in that sense.

Anxious
Ancient Greece · Socrates

The unexamined life is not worth living.

A journal is only honest
when it's truly private.

Everything you speak into untangle stays on your device. Your notes, transcripts, and insights never touch a server. An account exists only for billing. Your inner life belongs to you — and only you.

Free to download  ·  iOS & Android

Begin the conversation
with yourself.

Five minutes of speaking out loud can reveal what months of overthinking couldn't.

Get it onGoogle Play

No subscription needed to start  ·  Private by default
